title: User Badges System for Settings and Milestones
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to earn and view badges that represent milestones in my onboarding and tool utilization, so that I feel recognized for my progress and engagement.
description:
Introduce a badges system within the user settings area. The Settings page is renamed to 'Settings & Badges' with a new 'Badges' section. Badges represent milestones in onboarding, training, and tool utilization, tying education modules together with the product.

Each badge has metadata:
- Badge image (artwork)
- Badge tooltip for the end user
- Badge article URL (link to related educational content)
- Badge video URL (opens a congratulatory or educational video in a modal)

The first badge is 'Early Explorer'. Badge artwork will be provided. A badge for first metric creation has been prototyped.
acceptance_criteria: A Badges section is visible in the user settings area
Settings page is renamed to 'Settings & Badges'
Each badge displays an image, tooltip, and optional links to articles and videos
The 'Early Explorer' badge is the first available badge
Badge article URLs open the linked content
Badge video URLs open a modal with the video
Badges are awarded based on milestone triggers (e.g., first metric added)
ux_changes: New Badges section in Settings page with badge images, tooltips, article links, and video modals. Settings renamed to 'Settings & Badges'.
middleware_changes: Badge assignment logic to trigger badges based on user milestones (e.g., first metric added, early explorer status).
data_model_changes: Badge model with fields for image, tooltip, article URL, video URL, and user-badge association table for tracking earned badges.
implementation_notes: Initial prototype shows a badge awarded for first metric creation. Badge artwork for 'Early Explorer' to be provided. The system is designed to be extensible for future badges tied to training and education modules.
comment_summary: Developer asked for clarification comparing badges to Upwork's tier system. A prototype badge for first metric creation was demonstrated with a screenshot. The feature is in review.
